visa_types.documents_required:
Travel health insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased it yet, please purchase during the online appointment or at VAC.
Appointment for documents submission in Service Visa Center.
 Dear Customers,
To experience good customer service, applicants have to make an appointment to apply for a visa at the Austria Visa Application Centre in Moscow. Please
                                                                            make an appointment (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/rus/en/aut/book-an-appointment) online. Please note that an appointment is mandatory to apply
                                                                            for a visa at Austria Visa Application Centers in Russia.
This list is intended to help you compile the documents for your visa application. Please note that the Embassy may ask you for additional documents or further information if this
                                                                            is necessary to assess your application.
Main Rules: 
- Dear Customers: We would like to pay your attention to documents submission. Submission process can be strictly realized by personal presence or through second persons: Your relatives
                                                                                if they present documents for relationship.
- Documents submission can be realized if a person has at least one Schengen Visas irrespective of the country of issuance in that case documents can be accepted by your relatives
                                                                                if they present documents for relationship.
Original documents and photocopies
When you plan to visit the visa service center to submit your visa application, you must carry all original documents and their photocopies. Visa service center staff will accept
                                                                            photocopies only after checking the original documents. 
Please ensure that the photocopies are of good quality and can be read easily.
If you do not provide all required documents, it might be difficult for the embassy staff to assess your application accurately, which may result in your application being delayed
                                                                            or refused. 
Translated documents
You also have to provide a translation of any supporting documents that are not in English or German. If you do not provide a translation of your supporting documents, it might be difficult
                                                                            for the staff of Austrian Embassy in Moscow to assess your application accurately, which may result in your application being delayed or refused.
Each translated document must contain:
- confirmation that it is an accurate translation of the original document
- the date of the translation
- the translator's full name and signature
- the translator's contact details
Note: You can also make the translations of any document if you are able to do so. Translation must be the true copy of the original document. You must understand that if the translation is not correct then it may result your application being delayed/ refused.
When can you provide your documents?
Austrian embassy will only consider documents submitted with your application. You cannot submit documents after you have completed your application at the service centre, unless
                                                                            our staff asks you for them.
If you do not have a document
You should check that you have all the relevant documents that you want the visa officer to see before you apply for a visa. This is because it is your responsibility to satisfy the
                                                                            visa officer that you qualify for the visa that you have applied for.
If you do not have a document that is listed here, or that you think might be relevant to your application, you should explain why you do not have it. You must not provide a false
                                                                            document. Staff at the visa service centre will put his/her remarks on the checklist in case of deficiency of required documents.
If you provide a false document, it may result of your application being refused. 
Note
Before submission kindly check if Austria remains the main state of destination in Schengen area. 
If You are a citizen of Russian Federation and you are not leaving an airport area, You won’t need a transit visa.
Important:
Each applicant should prepare a common and full package of documents. 
The list of required documents for a visa. 
Before documents submission in Austria Service Center, You ought to fill in an agreement on personal data processing.
                                                                                                                                                
- Application form
One (1) completed and signed visa application form duly filled in English or German. Application
                                                                                    form have to be signed by a person who submits documents. 
Application form for children:
Each child has to have a separate application form even if his or her name entered on mother’s passport. Minors (before 18) must have a signature in application form at least one of the parents/legal guardian.
If you can’t prepare the Visa Application form by yourself, our manager can fill the Visa application form for you.
- Travel document
Valid passport with 2 expanded empty pages or 1 sheet, the copy of first page of foreign passport with personal data. The passport validity of at least three (3) months after
                                                                                    expiry of the visa.
The copies of all Schengen Visas for the whole period of live.
If you need to annul your previous Schengen visa please fill in an annulation form.
- Two photographs 
Requirements: Your visa application photos must meet certain criteria regarding size and content. Submitting photos that do not meet these criteria can delay the visa application
                                                                                    process. 
If you haven’t opportunity to make photo in advance, you can make photo in our VAC.
- Insurance
Travel, health and accident insurance for the entire period with a Minimum coverage of € 30.000, - and valid throughout the Schengen area. Insurance policies have to be purchased
                                                                                    from the companies accredited and are listed on the website of Austrian Embassy in Moscow. Very Important that the insurance policy covers all risks.
 You can make an insurance during the online appointment or in our VAC 
- Copy of the internal passport (not for children less than 14 years old): pages concerning the applicant's biodata, foreign passport(s) issued to
                                                                                him/her, registration in Russia and page 14 with or without marks.
- In cases of minors.
- Copy of the birth certificate.
- Consent of the parental authority or legal guardian done by a notary public, only if the minor travels alone or only with one parent. In
                                                                                        cases of a single parent holding the parental authority alone (i.e. when the other parent has deceased or has been deprived of custody, the death certificate or the
                                                                                        court decree is requested). Consent letter from parents should be original plus copy.
- Copy of the valid Schengen visa of the parent(s) travelling with the minor in case the visas are not applied for at the same time. If documents
                                                                                        are submitted by parents for their child, and each parent has opened Schengen visa in that case they should add booking where should be mentioned surnames, names of
                                                                                        guests who will stay in a hotel, tickets of flight and departure.
- In cases of third-country nationals proof of legal residence in the Russian Federation in line with federal legislation (e.g. residence permit, long-term Russian visa and
                                                                                    registration), valid for at least three months after the planned return from the Schengen area or a document proving that the applicant has applied for a renewal of the
                                                                                    proof of legal residence
If the applicant is not a resident in the Russian Federation, he/she needs to submit proof of legal presence in the Russian Federation (e.g. visa) and a written justification
                                                                                    for submitting the visa application in the Russian Federation instead of the country of residence
- Round trip tickets/reservation/itinerary of travel with exact dates
- Additional required documents depending on the purpose of travel:
- Members of official delegations:
- Letter (e.g. verbal note) issued by a competent Russian authority (e.g. Russian Ministry of Foreign Affairs) confirming that the applicant is a member of its delegation travelling to the Schengen Member State.
- Copy of the official invitation.
- Employees travelling on business: .
- Invitation from your business partner in Austria.
In principle the original invitation should be presented. The visa section will start the examination on the basis of copies (fax, scans), but may – if necessary – ask for the original.
If no invitation can be presented, other proof of purpose of travel (e.g. information on participation in conference, entrance ticket to trade fair, business correspondence, programme of the business trip).
In case not already sufficiently demonstrated in the invitation, proof of employment.
In order to avoid time-consuming requests for additional information or documentation, it is highly recommended – in particular to first-time applicants – to provide Information on the Russian employer, e.g. number of entry in Russian trade register
- Self-employed people travelling on business:
- Invitation from your business partner in Austria.
In principle the original invitation should be presented. The visa section will start the examination on the basis of copies (fax, scans), but may – if necessary – ask for the original.
If no invitation can be presented, other proof of purpose of travel (e.g. information on participation in conference, entrance ticket to trade fair, business correspondence, programme of the business trip).
- Proof of economic activity (e.g. 2NDFL or 3NDFL form, extract from the trade/fiscal registry or certificate for individual business) or originals of bank statement(s) covering at least three preceding months.
- Drivers (international cargo):
- Written request from the Russian national association of carriers (ASMAP or RAS) stating the purpose, duration and frequency of trips.
- Invitation from your business partner in Austria.
In principle the original invitation should be presented. The visa section will start the examination on the basis of copies (fax, scans), but may – if necessary – ask for the original.
- Copy of license for international transportation of your employer.
- Copy of your driver’s license.
- Copy of your employment history (trudovaya knizhka) or copy of your contract of employment.
- Drivers (international passengers):
- Written request from the Russian national association of carriers (ASMAP or RAS) stating the purpose, duration and frequency of trips.
- Invitation from your business partner in Austria.
In principle the original invitation should be presented. The visa section will start the examination on the basis of copies (fax, scans), but may – if necessary – ask for the original.
- Copy of license for international passenger transportation of your employer.
- Officially certified copy and simple copy of license to operate an international bus line issued from a Austrian district council addressed to your employer.
- Copy of license issued by the Russian transportation ministry for the same bus line.
- Copy of your driver’s license.
- Copy of your employment history (trudovaya knizhka) or copy of your contract of employment.
- Members of train, refrigerator and locomotive crews for up to 90 days/year:
- Written request from the competent Russian railway company (OAO-RZD and its branches and
- Proof of employment or copy of your employment history (trudovaya knizhka).
- Journalists for up to 90 days/year:
- A certificate or other document issued by a professional organisation proving that you are a qualified journalist.
- Letter from your employer stating that the purpose of the journey is to carry out journalistic work.
- Freelance journalists for up to 90 days/year:
- Proof of journalistic activity (e.g. a press card or work contract)
- Proof of economic activity/financial means (e.g. 2NDFL or 3NDFL form, excerpt from the trade/fiscal registry, certificate for individual business, original of bank statement(s) covering at least three preceding months).
- Workers posted to Austria for specific short-term activities:
This applies to persons who are sent to Austria by their employer, whose registered office is outside Austria, for up to three months within a period of twelve months for one of the following activities:
To erect and assemble machines, systems and software which have been ordered from the employer and to provide instruction in their operation, service or repair:
- Original proof of employment
- Invitation from the Austrian business partner
In principle the original invitation should be presented. The visa section will start the examination on the basis of copies (fax, scans), but may – if necessary – ask for the original.
- Supply contract and import/export certificate for the machines, systems and software delivered to Austria
- Confirmation of notification of the Federal Employment Agency about the employment
To accept machines, systems or other objects acquired for the Russian employer and/or receive instructions in their operation:
- Original proof of employment
- Invitation from the Austrian business partner
In principle the original invitation should be presented. The visa section will start the examination on the basis of copies (fax, scans), but may – if necessary – ask for the original.
- Supply contract and import/export certificate for the machines, systems or other objects acquired in Austria
To dismantle acquired, used systems for the purpose of re-erecting them in Russia:
- Original proof of employment
- Invitation from the Austrian business partner
In principle the original invitation should be presented. The visa section will start the examination on the basis of copies (fax, scans), but may – if necessary – ask for the original.
- Sales contract and proof of financing of the acquired, used systems
- Confirmation of notification of the Federal Employment Agency about the employment
To erect, dismantle and manage the company's own trade fair stands or the trade fair stands of a foreign company which is resident in Russia:
- Original proof of employment
- Proof of trade fair taking place, lease contract for trade fair stand
To participate in training as part of export delivery and licensing agreements:
- Original proof of employment
- Invitation from the Austrian business partner
In principle the original invitation should be presented. The visa section will start the examination on the basis of copies (fax, scans), but may – if necessary – ask for the original.
- Detailed training plan, proof of existing export delivery and/or license agreement
- Scientists with scholarship from public funds or visiting scientists invited by a university or other public research facility (up to three months)
Invitation or work contract from public university or other public research facility and/or promise of scholarship of the Austrian scientific organisation with designated university or research facility and a copy thereof.
In principle the original invitation should be presented. The visa section will start the examination on the basis of copies (fax, scans), but may – if necessary – ask for the original.
- Letter of deployment of your Russian university.
- In case of accompanying spouse or children: Marriage certificate and/or birth certificate for the children with a copy of each.
- Holiday jobs and work placements (up to three months)
Applies to:
Students and pupils planning a holiday job of up to three months per year in Austria if the work has been arranged by the International Placement Service (ZAV).
Students planning a work placement of up to three months as part of a proven international exchange programme involving associations, public authorities or student organisations for students or graduates of foreign universities in agreement with the International Placement Service (ZAV).
Please note that for other kinds of work placements (longer than three months or without approval of the ZAV), you will have to apply for a national visa.
- Placement letter of the ZAV and a copy thereof.
- Recent certificate of study

visa_types.processing_time:
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 15 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period. 
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
If your travel to Austria is for one of the following purpose, you are eligible to apply for this visa.
- Members of official delegations
- Employees travelling on business
- Self-employed people travelling on business
- Drivers (international cargo)
- Members of train, refrigerator and locomotive crews for up to 90 days/year
- Journalists
- Freelance journalists
- Workers posted to Germany for specific short-term activities
- Scientists with scholarship from public funds or visiting scientists invited by a university or other public research facility
- Holiday jobs and work placements
Description 
If you plan to go to Austria on a business  trip for a period not to  exceed 90 days and your country is not on the visa-free
                                                                            list, you need a Schengen travel  visa (Visa C). This visa is not a work permit. 
Please note that in order to apply for a travel visa at the Austrian Embassy, your only  destination or your main  destination                                                                            (longest duration of stay) must be Austria. In the absence of a main destination, Austria must be your first point
                                                                            of entry into the Schengen zone.
Appointment for documents  submission in Service Visa Center.
To experience good customer service, applicants are requested to make an appointment to apply for a Schengen visa to visit Austria at the Austria Visa Application Centre in Moscow. To make an appointment, please call on +7(499)703-32-47, (during working days and hours). Please note, an appointment is mandatory to apply for a Schengen visa at the Austria Visa Application Centre.
Please pay Your attention  to necessity of appointment.
Main Rules:
- Dear Guests: We would like to pay your attention to documents submission. Submission process can be strictly realized by personal presence or through second persons: Your relatives
                                                                                if they present documents for relationship.
- Documents submission can be strictly realized if person Schengen Visa, in that case documents can be accepted by your relatives if they present documents for relationship.
Important: 
Before documents submission in Austria Service Center, You ought to fill in an agreement on personal  data processing. 
If you are a sponsor, you ought to fill in a sponsor letter.
Important:
Before giving the  sponsorship letter, attach a first page copy of national passport.
 If you need to annul your previous Schengen visa please fill in an annulation form.  
If you travel by car, it’s recommended to attach a route list to common list of documents. 
Note
 Before submission kindly check if your dates in Austria are longer than to other countries of Schengen area.

- Proof of economic activity/financial means (e.g. 2NDFL or 3NDFL form, excerpt from the trade/fiscal registry, certificate for individual business, original of bank statement(s) covering at least three preceding months).
- Workers posted to Austria for specific short-term activities:
This applies to persons who are sent to Austria by their employer, whose registered office is outside Austria, for up to three months within a period of twelve months for one of the following activities:
To erect and assemble machines, systems and software which have been ordered from the employer and to provide instruction in their operation, service or repair:
- Original proof of employment
- Invitation from the Austrian business partner
In principle the original invitation should be presented. The visa section will start the examination on the basis of copies (fax, scans), but may – if necessary – ask for the original.
- Supply contract and import/export certificate for the machines, systems and software delivered to Austria
- Confirmation of notification of the Federal Employment Agency about the employment
To accept machines, systems or other objects acquired for the Russian employer and/or receive instructions in their operation:
- Original proof of employment
- Invitation from the Austrian business partner
In principle the original invitation should be presented. The visa section will start the examination on the basis of copies (fax, scans), but may – if necessary – ask for the original.
- Supply contract and import/export certificate for the machines, systems or other objects acquired in Austria
To dismantle acquired, used systems for the purpose of re-erecting them in Russia:
- Original proof of employment
- Invitation from the Austrian business partner
In principle the original invitation should be presented. The visa section will start the examination on the basis of copies (fax, scans), but may – if necessary – ask for the original.
- Sales contract and proof of financing of the acquired, used systems
- Confirmation of notification of the Federal Employment Agency about the employment
To erect, dismantle and manage the company's own trade fair stands or the trade fair stands of a foreign company which is resident in Russia:
- Original proof of employment
- Proof of trade fair taking place, lease contract for trade fair stand
To participate in training as part of export delivery and licensing agreements:
- Original proof of employment
- Invitation from the Austrian business partner
In principle the original invitation should be presented. The visa section will start the examination on the basis of copies (fax, scans), but may – if necessary – ask for the original.
- Detailed training plan, proof of existing export delivery and/or license agreement
- Scientists with scholarship from public funds or visiting scientists invited by a university or other public research facility (up to three months)
Invitation or work contract from public university or other public research facility and/or promise of scholarship of the Austrian scientific organisation with designated university or research facility and a copy thereof.
In principle the original invitation should be presented. The visa section will start the examination on the basis of copies (fax, scans), but may – if necessary – ask for the original.
- Letter of deployment of your Russian university.
- In case of accompanying spouse or children: Marriage certificate and/or birth certificate for the children with a copy of each.
- Holiday jobs and work placements (up to three months)
Applies to:
Students and pupils planning a holiday job of up to three months per year in Austria if the work has been arranged by the International Placement Service (ZAV).
Students planning a work placement of up to three months as part of a proven international exchange programme involving associations, public authorities or student organisations for students or graduates of foreign universities in agreement with the International Placement Service (ZAV).
Please note that for other kinds of work placements (longer than three months or without approval of the ZAV), you will have to apply for a national visa.
- Placement letter of the ZAV and a copy thereof.
- Recent certificate of study

## Photo Specifications
Overview
When applying for a visa, you are required to bring 2 photographs as part of completing and submitting your visa application. 
 If you haven’t opportunity to make photo in advance, you can make photo in our VAC.
Photo Requirements
General requirements
- Photo for each visa applicant must be an un mounted, full face photo as described below
- Photo must be taken within the last 6 months, for small children within the last 3 months..
Photo size: Photo submitted must measure as below mentioned
- 35 mm x 45 mm with the head centered in the frame.
- The head (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should measure about 30 mm
Photo Appearance
- The photo must be a full-face front view in which the visa applicant is facing the camera directly, with closed mouth, without smile.
- The face should cover about 70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The photo must be in color, made on matte or glossy paper and have only a white background. Photos with dark, busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted.
- In general, the applicant's head, including both face and hair, should be shown from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from hairline side-to-side.
                                                                                It is preferable that ears be exposed.
- Sunglasses or other wear, which detracts from the face, are not acceptable unless required for medical reasons (an eye patch, for example).
- A photo depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or veil that does not permit adequate identification is not acceptable.
Attention: Please follow these instructions carefully. If photos presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete
More Information
For more information about visa application photo specifications, visit click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4JKELG9sj6WOHYM1Qlkdil/73b6265005fa368665d8014efda27816/photo-specification.Pdf)

## Processing Time
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 15 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period. 
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.

Note
Minorities (before 18) must have 2 signatures in application form from relatives: mother and father. 
Application form filling

                                                                            is necessary to assess your application.

                                                                                if they present documents for relationship.

                                                                                if they present documents for relationship.
Original documents and photocopies
When you plan to visit the visa service center to submit your visa application, you must carry all original documents and their photocopies. Visa service center staff will accept
                                                                            photocopies only after checking the original documents.

If you do not provide all required documents, it might be difficult for the embassy staff to assess your application accurately, which may result in your application being delayed
                                                                            or refused. 
Translated documents

                                                                            for the staff of Austrian Embassy in Moscow to assess your application accurately, which may result in your application being delayed or refused.
Each translated document must contain:
- confirmation that it is an accurate translation of the original document
- the date of the translation
- the translator's full name and signature
- the translator's contact details
Note: You can also make the translations of any document if you are able to do so. Translation must be the true copy of the original document. You must understand that if the translation is not correct then it may result your application being delayed/ refused.

Austrian embassy will only consider documents submitted with your application. You cannot submit documents after you have completed your application at the service centre, unless
                                                                            our staff asks you for them.
If you do not have a document

                                                                            visa officer that you qualify for the visa that you have applied for.

                                                                            document. Staff at the visa service centre will put his/her remarks on the checklist in case of deficiency of required documents.
If you provide a false document, it may result of your application being refused. 
Note
Before submission kindly check if Austria remains the main state of destination in Schengen area. 
If You are a citizen of Russian Federation and you are not leaving an airport area, You won’t need a transit visa.

Each applicant should prepare a common and full package of documents.

                                                                                    form have to be signed by a person who submits documents.

If you can’t prepare the Visa Application form by yourself, our manager can fill the Visa application form for you.

Valid passport with 2 expanded empty pages or 1 sheet, the copy of first page of foreign passport with personal data. The passport validity of at least three (3) months after
                                                                                    expiry of the visa.

If you need to annul your previous Schengen visa please fill in an annulation form .

                                                                                    process. 
If you haven’t opportunity to make photo in advance, you can make photo in our VAC.

Travel, health and accident insurance for the entire period with a Minimum coverage of € 30.000, - and valid throughout the Schengen area. Insurance policies have to be purchased
                                                                                    from the companies accredited and are listed on the website of Austrian Embassy in Moscow. Very Important that the insurance policy covers all risks.
 You can make an insurance during the online appointment or in our VAC 
- Copy of the internal passport
 
(not for children less than 14 years old): pages concerning the applicant's biodata, foreign passport(s) issued to him/her, registration in Russia and page 14 with or without marks.
- In cases of minors.

done by a notary public, only if the minor travels alone or only with one parent. - 35 mm x 45 mm with the head centered in the frame.
- The head (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should measure about 30 mm

- The photo must be a full-face front view in which the visa applicant is facing the camera directly, with closed mouth, without smile.
- The face should cover about 70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The photo must be in color, made on matte or glossy paper and have only a white background. Photos with dark, busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted.

visa_types.name: National D Visa
visa_types.overview:
If you intend to stay in Austria for more than 90 days and less than six months ( student exchange, short term study) per year and you are neither EU or EFTA citizen, you require an Austrian national visa (Visa D).
According to the Schengen Visa Code the holder of a national visa issued by Austria or other Schengen-country is entitled to travel in other Schengen-countries for a maximum of 90 days within a 6 months period (i.e. the maximum validity of the Austrian visa D).
Note: Visa Service Centres accept applications for all types D visa, including ones in order to receive the Residence Permit in Austria, documents for which were already submitted at Embassy of Austria in advance. For the additional information on D visas you can consult the website of the Embassy of Austria in Moscow (https://www.bmeia.gv.at/oeb-moskau/reisen-nach-oesterreich/einreise-und-aufenthalt/visum/)
Application form for D visa is also available on the website of the Embassy of Austria in Moscow.

Note: Visa Service Centres accept applications for all types D visa, including ones in order to receive the Residence Permit in Austria, documents for which were already submitted at Embassy of Austria in advance. For the additional information on D visas you can consult the website of the Embassy of Austria in Moscow (https://www.bmeia.gv.at/oeb-moskau/reisen-nach-oesterreich/einreise-und-aufenthalt/visum/)
Application form for D visa is also available on the website of the Embassy of Austria in Moscow.

visa_types.documents_required:
Travel health insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased it yet, please purchase during the online appointment or at VAC.
Appointment for documents submission in Service Visa Center.
 Dear Customers,
To experience good customer service, applicants have to make an appointment to apply for a visa at the Austria Visa Application Centre in Moscow. Please
                                                                            make an appointment (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/rus/en/aut/book-an-appointment) online. Please note that an appointment is mandatory to apply
                                                                            for a visa at Austria Visa Application Centers in Russia.
This list is intended to help you compile the documents for your visa application. Please note that the Embassy may ask you for additional documents or further information if this
                                                                            is necessary to assess your application.
Main Rules: 
- Dear Customers: We would like to pay your attention to documents submission. Submission process can be strictly realized by personal presence or through second persons: Your relatives
                                                                                if they present documents for relationship.
- Documents submission can be realized if a person has at least one Schengen Visas irrespective of the country of issuance in that case documents can be accepted by your relatives
                                                                                if they present documents for relationship.

Original documents and photocopies
When you plan to visit the visa service center to submit your visa application, you must carry all original documents and their photocopies. Visa service center staff will accept
                                                                            photocopies only after checking the original documents. 
Please ensure that the photocopies are of good quality and can be read easily.
If you do not provide all required documents, it might be difficult for the embassy staff to assess your application accurately, which may result in your application being delayed
                                                                            or refused. 

Translated documents
You also have to provide a translation of any supporting documents that are not in English or German. If you do not provide a translation of your supporting documents, it might be difficult
                                                                            for the staff of Austrian Embassy in Moscow to assess your application accurately, which may result in your application being delayed or refused.
Each translated document must contain:
- confirmation that it is an accurate translation of the original document
- the date of the translation
- the translator's full name and signature
- the translator's contact details
Note: You can also make the translations of any document if you are able to do so. Translation must be the true copy of the original document. You must understand that if the translation is not correct then it may result your application being delayed/ refused.
When can you provide your documents?
Austrian embassy will only consider documents submitted with your application. You cannot submit documents after you have completed your application at the service centre, unless
                                                                            our staff asks you for them.

If you do not have a document
You should check that you have all the relevant documents that you want the visa officer to see before you apply for a visa. This is because it is your responsibility to satisfy the
                                                                            visa officer that you qualify for the visa that you have applied for.
If you do not have a document that is listed here, or that you think might be relevant to your application, you should explain why you do not have it. You must not provide a false
                                                                            document. Staff at the visa service centre will put his/her remarks on the checklist in case of deficiency of required documents.
If you provide a false document, it may result of your application being refused. 

Note
Before submission kindly check if Austria remains the main state of destination in Schengen area. 
If You are a citizen of Russian Federation and you are not leaving an airport area, You won’t need a transit visa.

Important:
Each applicant should prepare a common and full package of documents. 
The list of required documents for a visa. 
Before documents submission in Austria Service Center, You ought to fill in an agreement on personal data processing.
                                                                                                                                                
- Application form
One (1) completed and signed visa application form duly filled in English or German. Application
                                                                                    form have to be signed by a person who submits documents. 
Application form for children:
Each child has to have a separate application form even if his or her name entered on mother’s passport. Minors (before 18) must have a signature in application form at least one of the parents/legal guardian.
If you can’t prepare the Visa Application form by yourself, our manager can fill the Visa application form for you.
- Travel document
Valid passport with 2 expanded empty pages or 1 sheet, the copy of first page of foreign passport with personal data. The passport validity of at least three (3) months after
                                                                                    expiry of the visa.
The copies of all Schengen Visas for the whole period of live.
If you need to annul your previous Schengen visa please fill in an annulation form.
- Two photographs 
Requirements: Your visa application photos must meet certain criteria regarding size and content. Submitting photos that do not meet these criteria can delay the visa application
                                                                                    process. 
If you haven’t opportunity to make photo in advance, you can make photo in our VAC.
- Insurance
Travel, health and accident insurance for the entire period with a Minimum coverage of € 30.000, - and valid throughout the Schengen area. Insurance policies have to be purchased
                                                                                    from the companies accredited and are listed on the website of Austrian Embassy in Moscow. Very Important that the insurance policy covers all risks.
 You can make an insurance during the online appointment or in our VAC 
- Copy of the internal passport  (not for children less than 14 years old): pages concerning the applicant's biodata, foreign passport(s) issued to
                                                                                him/her, registration in Russia and page 14 with or without marks.
- In cases of minors.
- Copy of the birth certificate.
- Consent of the parental authority or legal guardian done by a notary public, only if the minor travels alone or only with one parent. In
                                                                                        cases of a single parent holding the parental authority alone (i.e. when the other parent has deceased or has been deprived of custody, the death certificate or the
                                                                                        court decree is requested). Consent letter from parents should be original plus copy.
- Copy of the valid Schengen visa of the parent(s) travelling with the minor in case the visas are not applied for at the same time. If documents
                                                                                        are submitted by parents for their child, and each parent has opened Schengen visa in that case they should add booking where should be mentioned surnames, names of
                                                                                        guests who will stay in a hotel, tickets of flight and departure.
- In cases of third-country nationals proof of legal residence in the Russian Federation in line with federal legislation (e.g. residence permit, long-term Russian visa and
                                                                                    registration), valid for at least three months after the planned return from the Schengen area or a document proving that the applicant has applied for a renewal of the
                                                                                    proof of legal residence
If the applicant is not a resident in the Russian Federation, he/she needs to submit proof of legal presence in the Russian Federation (e.g. visa) and a written justification
                                                                                    for submitting the visa application in the Russian Federation instead of the country of residence
- Round trip tickets/reservation/itinerary of travel with exact dates
Additional required documents:
Close relatives visiting Russian citizens residing legally in Austria:
- Invitation from the host including the full address of the intended stay, either informal with an officially certified signature (or copy of host’s passport)
- Proof of the legal residence of the inviting person in Austria (copy of residence permit) as well as proof of family tie (e.g. copy of birth or marriage certificates).
- Proof of itinerary (e.g. a confirmation of the booking of an organised trip or any other appropriate document indicating the envisaged travel plans such as a return
                                                                                                ticket, paid flight ticket, if not available, adequate written description of the planned journey.
- Proof of financial means (e.g. proof of employment with salary information, original of bank/credit card statement(s) covering at least three preceding months
                                                                                                or proof of sponsorship).
- Indication of will to return (e.g. certificate of employment or proof of real estate property in Russia).
Close relatives visiting EU (EEA & Swiss) citizens:
- Invitation from the host including the full address of the intended stay, either informal with an officially certified signature (or copy of host’s passport)
- Proof of the nationality of the inviting person and proof of family tie (e.g. copy of passport or certificate of citizenship; birth certificate, marriage certificate).
- Proof of financial means (e.g. proof of employment with salary information, original of bank/credit card statement(s) covering at least three preceding months,
                                                                                                proof of real estate property in Russia or proof of sponsorship).
- Indication of will to return e.g. certificate of employment or proof of real estate property in Russia.

                                                                            country is not on the visa-free list, you need a Schengen travel visa (Visa C).

Please pay Your attention  to necessity of appointment.

- Dear Guests: We would like to pay your attention to documents submission. Submission process can be strictly realized by personal presence or through second persons: Your relatives
                                                                                if they present documents for relationship.
- Documents submission can be strictly realized if person Schengen Visa, in that case documents can be accepted by your relatives if they present documents for relationship.
Important:

Important:

If you need to annul your previous Schengen visa please fill in an annulation form. If you travel by car, it’s recommended to attach the annulation form to common list of documents.

- Photo for each visa applicant must be an un mounted, full face photo as described below

- 35 mm x 45 mm with the head centered in the frame.
- The head (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should measure about 30 mm

- Sunglasses or other wear, which detracts from the face, are not acceptable unless required for medical reasons (an eye patch, for example).

visa_types.processing_time:
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 15 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period. 
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.

Overview
If your travel to Austria is for one of the following purpose, you are eligible to apply for this visa.
- Tourists
- Private visitors (excluding family visits)
- Property owners in the Schengen area and their close relatives
- Visitors of military and civil burial grounds
- Medical Treatment
Description 
If you plan to go to Austria as a tourist for a period not to exceed 90 days and your
                                                                            country is not on the visa-free list, you need a Schengen travel visa (Visa C). This visa is not a work permit.  
Please note that in order to apply for a travel visa at the Austrian Embassy, your only destination or your main destination                                                                            (longest duration of stay) must be Austria. In the absence of a main destination (e.g. same duration of stay in two or more countries) you can apply
                                                                            at any of these countries but it is recommended that you apply at the country of first entry because your passport will show the entry stamp of your first destination only

To make an appointment, please call on +7(499)703-32-47, (during working days and hours). Please note, an appointment is mandatory to apply for a Schengen visa at the Austria Visa Application Centre.
Please pay Your  attention to necessity of appointment.
Main Rules:
- Dear Guests: We would like to pay your attention to documents submission. Submission process can be strictly realized by personal presence or through second persons: Your relatives
                                                                                if they present documents for relationship.
- Documents submission can be strictly realized if person Schengen Visa, in that case documents can be accepted by your relatives if they present documents for relationship.
Important: 
Before documents submission in Austria Service Center, You ought to fill in an agreement on personal  data processing. 
If you are a sponsor, you ought to fill in a sponsor letter.
Important:
Before giving the  sponsorship letter, attach a first page copy of national passport.
 If you need to annul your previous Schengen visa please fill in an annulation form.  
If you travel by car, it’s recommended to attach a route list to common list of documents.

 Dear Customers,
To experience good customer service, applicants have to make an appointment to apply for a visa at the Austria Visa Application Centre in Moscow. Please

                                                                            for a visa at Austria Visa Application Centers in Russia.
This list is intended to help you compile the documents for your visa application. Please note that the Embassy may ask you for additional documents or further information if this
                                                                            is necessary to assess your application.
Main Rules: 
- Dear Customers: We would like to pay your attention to documents submission. Submission process can be strictly realized by personal presence or through second persons: Your relatives
                                                                                if they present documents for relationship.

                                                                                if they present documents for relationship.
Original documents and photocopies
When you plan to visit the visa service center to submit your visa application, you must carry all original documents and their photocopies. Visa service center staff will accept
                                                                            photocopies only after checking the original documents. 
Please ensure that the photocopies are of good quality and can be read easily.
If you do not provide all required documents, it might be difficult for the embassy staff to assess your application accurately, which may result in your application being delayed
                                                                            or refused. 
Translated documents
You also have to provide a translation of any supporting documents that are not in English or German. If you do not provide a translation of your supporting documents, it might be difficult
                                                                            for the staff of Austrian Embassy in Moscow to assess your application accurately, which may result in your application being delayed or refused.
Each translated document must contain:
- confirmation that it is an accurate translation of the original document
- the date of the translation
- the translator's full name and signature
- the translator's contact details
Note: You can also make the translations of any document if you are able to do so. Translation must be the true copy of the original document. You must understand that if the translation is not correct then it may result your application being delayed/ refused.

Austrian embassy will only consider documents submitted with your application. You cannot submit documents after you have completed your application at the service centre, unless
                                                                            our staff asks you for them.
If you do not have a document
You should check that you have all the relevant documents that you want the visa officer to see before you apply for a visa. This is because it is your responsibility to satisfy the
                                                                            visa officer that you qualify for the visa that you have applied for.
If you do not have a document that is listed here, or that you think might be relevant to your application, you should explain why you do not have it. You must not provide a false
                                                                            document. Staff at the visa service centre will put his/her remarks on the checklist in case of deficiency of required documents.
If you provide a false document, it may result of your application being refused. 
Note
Before submission kindly check if Austria remains the main state of destination in Schengen area. 
If You are a citizen of Russian Federation and you are not leaving an airport area, You won’t need a transit visa.

## Photo Specifications
Overview
When applying for a visa, you are required to bring 2 photographs as part of completing and submitting your visa application. 
 If you haven’t opportunity to make photo in advance, you can make photo in our VAC.
Photo Requirements
General requirements
- Photo for each visa applicant must be an un mounted, full face photo as described below
- Photo must be taken within the last 6 months, for small children within the last 3 months..
Photo size: Photo submitted must measure as below mentioned
- 35 mm x 45 mm with the head centered in the frame.
- The head (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should measure about 30 mm
Photo Appearance
- The photo must be a full-face front view in which the visa applicant is facing the camera directly, with closed mouth, without smile.
- The face should cover about 70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The photo must be in color, made on matte or glossy paper and have only a white background. Photos with dark, busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted.
- In general, the applicant's head, including both face and hair, should be shown from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from hairline side-to-side.
                                                                                It is preferable that ears be exposed.
- Sunglasses or other wear, which detracts from the face, are not acceptable unless required for medical reasons (an eye patch, for example).
- A photo depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or veil that does not permit adequate identification is not acceptable.
Attention: Please follow these instructions carefully. If photos presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete
More Information

## Processing Time
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 15 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period. 
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.
